key_points: Regional cultures developed because of differences in geography, language, and traditions across India
Local rulers and poets supported literature in regional languages such as Bengali and Tamil
The Bhakti movement helped spread religious ideas through devotional songs and poetry
Temples became important cultural centers where religious, social, and economic activities occurred
Miniature paintings developed in Rajput courts under the support of kings and nobles
examples: Chaitanya Mahaprabhu promoted devotion to Krishna through singing and dancing in Bengal
The Jagannath Temple in Puri became a major pilgrimage center in Odisha
Rajput rulers supported artists who created detailed miniature paintings
Epics such as the Ramayana were translated into regional languages
Pilgrimages helped spread cultural traditions between regions
formulas: Regional Culture → Language + Traditions + Religion + Geography
Bhakti Movement → Devotion to God through songs and poetry
Royal Patronage → Support from rulers for literature, art, and religion
exam_tips: Focus on the role of regional languages in spreading culture
Remember examples like Chaitanya and the Jagannath temple
Understand how rulers supported art and literature
Learn how pilgrimage centers helped spread traditions
